programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Old friends Dr Sloan and private investigator Joe Mannix are thrown together to unravel a case of corruption linked to an unsolved murder. Guest starring Mike Connors as the veteran TV detective

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan investigates the death of a popular country singer murdered in his dressing room minutes before he was due to make an acceptance speech. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Victoria Rowell

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Neuro-psychiatrist Mark Ryder employs a computer and Gerard's help to plan Richard Kimble's capture

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A young secretary is accused of murder after she is lured to a notorious playboy's beach house and he is later found stabbed to death

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A loan shark uses his influence over a minister's son to embezzle money from a charity telethon

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Horatio shoots a thief during a robbery but one of the criminal's accomplices still remains at large. As the team investigates, it turns out the stolen cash was counterfeit - leaving a question mark over where the real money went. Meanwhile, Horatio's personal life becomes more complicated. David Caruso stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy recruitment officer is murdered at a high school and the agents discover he had recently begun a relationship with a married teacher whose wife held a grudge against him. They also find out a student's father was vehemently opposed to his joining the military, and receive help from an unexpected quarter - Ducky's esteemed predecessor, Dr Magnus. Crime drama, guest starring comedian Bob Newhart

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A taxi driver recently released from prison is murdered just hours after sending an e-mail to an old alias that Callen used when he went undercover to investigate three marines who stole $5million. Resuming the persona, Callen goes in search of the gang, one of whom is killed in a drive-by shooting

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble finds an escaped prisoner who is dangerously ill, claiming to be innocent, and determined to gun down the man he blames for framing him

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A vindictive woman who refused to give her inventor husband a divorce is found murdered after trying to destroy his unique underwater sounding device

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A traumatised marine escapes from a psychiatric unit, convinced he is still fighting in Iraq. The agents believe his erratic behaviour indicates post-traumatic stress disorder and try to calmly talk him round before restraining him. However, blood tests reveal high levels of steroids in his system - meaning someone is using him as a human lab rat. Starring Lauren Holly, Sean Murray and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Alex takes on a gang of arsonists who try to murder the firefighters called out to extinguish their fires

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Tony reviews satellite footage of rebel forces in the Middle East and spots Kersey among a group of insurgents, concluding that Joe Westcott's fear of him was well-founded. Back at HQ, Abby creates a 3D computer model of the attack that occurred in Afghanistan and Joe dons a virtual-reality headset to relive the traumatic events. Will it jog his memory?

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen, Sam and the team spring into action when fellow agent Dom activates a distress signal. They track down his car, which is riddled with bullet holes, and Eric intercepts a signal from his phone, tracing it to a house that contains nothing but some used medical supplies and a dead body. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Muslim US Marine is found seemingly shot dead near a mosque that has been linked to an al-Qaeda recruiter. Ducky refuses to perform an autopsy out of respect for the man's religion, but Abby runs tests that reveal his demise was actually due to poisoning from a rare octopus venom. Meanwhile, McGee goes into the mosque intending to plant a concealed microphone - but discovers someone has had the same idea

programme.desc: Part one of two. Walker and his team deal with a gang whose extortion ends in the murder of a wealthy Dallas family and its staff, and a daughter of a rich businessman is kidnapped

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Teenager Lydia Wade manages to escape a gang of masked men trying to abduct her and her friend Rosie, the daughter of a US Navy commander. Lydia's Navy captain father David Wade was shot dead during the attack and her mother is overseas, so Ziva offers to look after the girl, while Abby tries to jog her memory by creating facial reconstructions of the attackers based on some grainy CCTV footage. DiNozzo and McGee find links on Rosie's laptop to possible suspect John 'Diesel' Stevens

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: After a mid-series break, the drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects returns. Sam and Callen investigate when five men wearing US Marine uniforms are found dead in a warehouse and discover they were killed by a lethal toxin that could be used as a bioweapon. Initial inquiries lead them to a former military chemist who now has Alzheimer's, while Callen recognises one of the victims as someone who used to work as hired muscle for the well-connected owner of a strip joint. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team set up a stake-out at a storage facility to catch a thief suspected of selling hi-tech Navy equipment on the black market, but end up witnessing a murder. To make matters worse, $90million worth of radar gear goes missing while they are distracted. Meanwhile, Ducky asks Abby to run a series of mysterious blood tests, raising suspicions that he is harbouring a secret

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey argues with Darryl about the lack of trust in their relationship, and visits a therapist for advice on how to resolve the issue. Comedy, starring Linda Robson

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon is convinced her 30th birthday is just the start of another three decades of misery, prompting Tracey to question the wisdom of throwing a party. Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Roger rescues probation officer Paul Brookes, who has been beaten and locked in the boot of his own car. Further investigations reveal the assailant's wife is a prisoner at Burnwood who claims she has been raped by Brookes. Gina turns up at court to give evidence, only to find the magistrate is an old flame, who promptly wipes the floor with her - before asking her out to dinner. Lewis finds a new home via the nurses' noticeboard at St Hugh's Hospital

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Joseph worries that Jac may be pregnant when she complains of feeling sick. Ric battles to save the lives of a security guard and the drug addict who stabbed her, while at the same time trying to placate the victim's fiance and the Press. After confessing to his priest about his feelings for an engaged woman, Linden learns Callie knows the truth about the night of the accident

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Luke feels responsible for a man's death at an Army barracks, unaware a senior officer failed to give him vital medical details. The love triangle between Baz, Dan and Charlie reaches a dramatic climax when she tells her husband all about romancing her ex. The girls get dressed up as movie characters for Nikki's hen night. When they bump into Jim's stag party, Luke and Lara waste no time getting it together in the hospital car park. Things are not quite so good between Fin and Comfort though, as he ends up carrying his depressed girlfriend home drunk

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Heaton puts his team in the picture about the possible closure of Sun Hill when rumours reach the local press. Mickey and Jo investigate the death of a young mother with a history of drug use and discover she has been clean for some time but has fallen prey to a ruthless loan shark. Roger and Dan talk to a pub landlord who has been attacked at a fancy dress stag night

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: The Jacksons have to find a new home while theirs is made safe after a fire next door. Michelle meet her new boss. Arthur's meeting is turned into a riot by troublemakers

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ricky and David cook a meal for Bianca and Sam respectively, but sabotage leads to a 4-way row. Michelle tries to arrange housing for the Jacksons. Pauline warns Phil about Ted

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Elizabeth enlists Prudence's help, and on the eve of her engagement party, Sarah confides to her aunt about a shameful secret. Meanwhile, Anna and Gino's relationship moves forward and James' treatment takes a disturbing turn. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Having barely had time to settle in at the Beeches, Will finds himself at odds with fellow doctor Sam over a dialysis patient who appears to be pressuring his younger half-brother into donating a kidney. Meanwhile, Alice makes a shocking discovery regarding the secret life of TV repair man Duggie. Medical drama, starring Joseph Millson and Simon Shepherd

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James's plans for an evening out are spoiled when a dog goes missing after an operation. Siegfried struggles to keep the budget under control, and Tristan's love life creates more trouble. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: A tattered shoebox filled with money arrives for Jeffrey, with instructions from Joe Maplin that it be used to bribe local councillors to not build a hospital next to the holiday camp. Comedy, starring Paul Shane

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Ted gets up to his usual tricks, using every money-raising fiddle in the book to fend off bailiffs threatening him with prison for failing to pay maintenance to his ex-wife. Paul Shane and Simon Cadell star in Jimmy Perry and David Croft's holiday camp comedy

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Relishing the social prestige it confers, Hyacinth decides that she and husband Richard should have a weekend at a golfing hotel so that she can keep up with the Major and his wife. Roy Clarke's suburban sitcom, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Tom and Smiler arrange an interesting blind date and Truly discovers his ex-wife's new husband is coming to town. Frank Thornton and Stephen Lewis star

programme.title: Kavanagh QC
programme.desc: A brother and sister stand accused of murdering their father and stepmother, and pleading for them proves to be the most complex assignment of Kavanagh's entire career. The task is made doubly difficult by the man's insistence on defending himself and a tragedy within his own family - and Julia's decision to leave chambers does not help either. Legal drama, starring John Thaw, Anna Chancellor and Art Malik

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Danny and Steve 'welcome' new boss Ted Case into the UCOS office in Sasha's absence, but for all his experience, keen eye for detail and a killer instinct for detecting liars, they aren't as sold on his superstitious quirks. While trying to work one another out, the team treads carefully in the case of a murdered vicar who received racist hate mail in the lead up to his death. With emotions running high, it's up to Ted to pull together his new unit and keep the peace in one of UCOS's most sensitive cases. Starring Larry Lamb, Denis Lawson and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: With Britain on the verge of a plague epidemic, the team must find a vaccine quickly. To that end, Harry orders the interrogation of spies from all nations likely to have it - a task he hands over to a trusted former colleague, Connie James. Each of the detainees is exposed to the virus - a tactic that helps them locate Zaf, and leads directly to secrets about the government's relationship with Russia. Gemma Jones joins the cast

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Heaton puts his team in the picture about the possible closure of Sun Hill when rumours reach the local press. Mickey and Jo investigate the death of a young mother with a history of drug use and discover she has been clean for some time but has fallen prey to a ruthless loan shark. Roger and Dan talk to a pub landlord who has been attacked at a fancy dress stag night

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: Jo Mills takes on the case of a worker killed on a building site, convinced that the managing director of the company should be held accountable for his neglect of health and safety legislation. However, the Lord Chancellor's department once again try to divert the course of justice to avoid embarrassment - and Deed's ex-wife is appointed counsel for the defence. Legal drama, starring Martin Shaw and Jenny Seagrove

programme.desc: Sam helps a lovestruck teenager deal with an embarrassing complaint, and pub landlord Mike Pullen ignores repeated warnings about his health, pushing himself to the limit to impress the brewery manager. Medical drama, starring Simon Shepherd, Joseph Millson and John Bowler

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Tristan earns himself a reputation as the best vet in Darrowby, while James and Siegfried take a look at new technology. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Quite a few feathers are ruffled as Joe Maplin pays a secret visit to the camp and decides Gladys should be promoted to head Yellowcoat - prompting her to sew on some stripes to display her superior rank. With Ruth Madoc, Simon Cadell, Su Pollard and Paul Shane

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Jeffrey's wife sends her new boyfriend to the camp to ask him for a divorce. When the professor is unwilling to co-operate, the pair begin digging for evidence. Classic comedy, starring Simon Cadell, Su Pollard and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth is thrown into dismay when her beloved father goes to the register office to be married, and things go from bad to worse when her neighbour Emmett comes face to face with her uncouth relatives, Daisy, Onslow and Rose

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: Kenneth Branagh stars as the world-weary Swedish detective in a fourth and final series of adaptations of Henning Mankell's novels. Wallander travels to Cape Town to investigate the disappearance of a Swedish woman. He uncovers a complex conspiracy behind the mystery, and is also confronted with a nation that has reached a turning point in its history

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Ted leads an investigation into the apparent suicide of a city trader who was embroiled in the cut-throat world of London's financial district in the 1980s. As the team delve deeper into the macho fraternity of 'The Wolfpack', they find a darker side to the debauchery, secret hand signals and all-nighters that bonded the traders together - including a strict hierarchy that might be worth killing for. Meanwhile, Steve tries to learn some tricks of the trade, amid his own financial crisis. Detective drama, starring Larry Lamb

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: When a plane is hijacked and crashed near an airbase in the UK it reveals the Americans were behind the false information which resulted in the disastrous Tehran operation. Drama, starring Hermione Norris

programme.desc: Sarah reveals her horrific secret to George, and their relationship is suddenly thrown into doubt. The Christmas soiree brings out Jack's intimacy issues, and Anna publicly humiliates Andrew. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: A pregnant woman's dilemma reminds Will of a tragedy in his own past, and Alex is forced to tackle her addiction while treating a victim of passive smoking. Drama, starring Simon Shepherd and Maggie O'Neill.

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Siegfried has to deal with an outbreak of foot-and-mouth disease, while the lack of gas masks for pets causes concern. Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy star

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Ted faces the potentially awkward task of hiring an X-rated film for the artistically inclined male campers. Classic comedy, starring Paul Shane

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Jeffrey decides Radio Maplin needs a new voice and Spike is seduced by a femme fatale. Classic comedy, starring Jeffrey Holland, Simon Cadell and Su Pollard

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth dreads the idea of attending Onslow's birthday celebrations. However, she changes her mind when she learns a limousine will be arriving to take her to the party. Starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.title: Maisie Raine
programme.desc: Mallory's inexperience causes a drug surveillance to go wrong - and jeopardises the life of informant Trudi into the bargain, causing Kyprianou to spring to her defence. Police drama, starring Pauline Quirke

programme.title: Frankie Drake Mysteries
programme.desc: Frankie is floored when a man claiming to be her half-brother shows up at the agency begging her to help his wrongly imprisoned friend. Period crime drama, starring Lauren Lee Smith as the first female private detective in 1920s Toronto

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: With Sasha back on fighting form, UCOS investigates the death of a talented cricket prodigy, who was the apple of his father's eye, yet loathed by his on-field team-mates. The death was recorded as misadventure but with new evidence pointing to infighting, over-zealous autograph hunters and extra marital affairs, UCOS are soon bowled over by the list of potential murder suspects. Meanwhile, wise to Steve's monetary problems, Danny appoints himself as his personal financial adviser. Detective drama, starring Larry Lamb

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Intelligence suggests an Algerian terrorist is planning an atrocity in London, so Adam returns to Ana for more information on the activities of her husband. Caught in the act, she kills an aide, leading Dariush to discover she is an MI5 mole. Meanwhile, Ros's Yalta contact informs her Bakhshi is not the culprit but the target of the expected attack, and working with the Americans proves difficult

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: As the Serious Organised Crime Agency begin to question Zain's undercover role with the Colombian gang, drugs mule Eva Garcia contacts her brother, blowing the security of the safe house and putting the detective in great danger from kingpin Alvarez. Honey argues with Will about pressing him to go on holiday together but before they can sort out their relationship she answers an emergency call from Eva

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: A doctor is accused of murdering a seriously ill patient with a morphine overdose, a case which echoes a dilemma in Deed's personal life and sets him on the path to potential ruin. Meanwhile, Charlie's HIV-positive friend finds herself in danger of losing her baby to Social Services, and calls on the judge for help. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Jenny Seagrove

programme.desc: News and weather

programme.title: Rybish
programme.desc: Despite the bad weather the Cefn Cilgwyn crew members are on top of their game as they receive a new computer system

programme.title: Dau Gi Bach
programme.desc: Skye brings happiness to a family who have been through tough times in Porthmadog, while Mabli Rhodd arrives at her new home in Blaenau Ffestiniog

programme.title: Un Bore Mercher
programme.desc: Unsettled by Rose's reappearance in her life, Faith struggles to keep everything together and focus on Osian's case. Evan decides to keep a closer eye on Faith's every move as Steve battles with his guilt. Crime drama, starring Eve Myles

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A chalet bungalow is given a transformation in Beccles, Norfolk, while developers also take on projects in Cwmaman in South Wales. Martin Roberts and Martel Maxwell present

programme.title: The Unknown Warrior 100
programme.desc: Huw Edwards presents live coverage from Westminster Abbey of a unique event marking the 100th anniversary of the burial of the Unknown Warrior. An Armistice Day ceremony encompassing the national moment of remembrance at 11.00 will reflect the original 1920 funeral of the Unknown Warrior, including a performance of the hymn Abide with Me, sung by Ruby Turner. Those in attendance include The Prince of Wales and The Duchess of Cornwall

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Emergency services rush to find a pregnant woman who has collapsed on a remote moor, and dash-cam footage captures the moment a driver falls asleep at the wheel

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the International Antiques and Collectors Fair in Ardingly, West Sussex, where experts Tim Weeks and Charles Hanson guide the reds and blue teams to find bargains that they hope will make a profit at auction. Meanwhile, Charlie meets one of the biggest stars of 1950s radio - ventriloquist doll Archie Andrews

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Rob helps a young PC gain confidence, and Jimmi uncovers Al's secret. Elsewhere, Shak is hiding something from Ruhma

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is in Dorset as she catches up with past house-hunters who have found a better quality of rural life near the coast

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades finds items to restore at Walsall Recycling Centre in the West Midlands, including a swivel chair and a sideboard

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and James Braxton search for antiques in Suffolk, before making their way to the Kent coast at Chatham and selling their items at auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball are joined by Matt Allwright for the weekly Watchdog investigation, and Matt Baker reveals how Team Rickshaw is getting on after another gruelling day of The One Show Rickshaw Challenge for Children in Need

programme.title: Life in Lockdown: A Special Film for BBC Children in Need
programme.desc: A powerful, uplifting and touching film which proves how brilliantly resilient children and young people can be in the face of a global crisis. This film shines a light on children and young people experiencing a variety of challenges whilst they also cope with the effects of the pandemic, and reveals how BBC Children in Need funded projects are providing vital support for families during this incredibly difficult time. Narrated by Emma Willis

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. First to arrive at the barn are sisters Christine Hopkins and Pauline Samways from Kidderminster, who bring in a miniature bicycle built by their grandfather. Elsewhere, silversmith Brenton West takes receipt of a small mosaic crucifix with an extraordinary story, and Steve Fletcher turns his attention to an army issued box kite. Finally, antique toy enthusiast David Burville sets to work restoring a Victorian acrobat toy

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Four more professional chefs arrive from across the nation to compete. In the infamous Skills Test, two chefs will try their hand at Marcus' test of sweetbreads with a rustic red wine sauce, before the second pair take on Monica's gazed sabayon fruit dessert. In the second round, the four chefs must impress the judges with two stand out Signature Dishes of their own creation, before two of the competitors are sent home

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: DJ Target, Krept and Konan ask the artists to write under pressure, before rapping about an issue they care for deeply

programme.title: Rap Trip: Underground Scenes Uncovered
programme.desc: FOS and Ransom travel to Leicester and Bradford to explore the British Asian rap scene, and attend a block party with fast cars and freestyles

programme.title: My Family, the Holocaust & Me with Robert Rinder
programme.desc: Part one of two. The barrister helps the second and third generations of British Jewish families affected by the Holocaust to retrace their relatives' footsteps and discover the full truth about what happened to them. Robert begins by meeting two British Jewish families who want to investigate what happened to their relatives, as well as exploring his own family's stories on both his mother's and his father's sides

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the development progress of auction-bought properties in Baddeley Green in Stoke, Rushden in Northampton and the village of Cwm near Ebbw Vale in Blaenau Gwent

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A convict claims he had an accident in the prison gym, a group of women stage a fall in a supermarket and when a Rolex gets ruined, the claimant has problems recalling his timings

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Stories of life in the balance, with Nick Knowles. A father-to-be has a horrific accident while mountainbiking with friends. A man braves a fire at home to search for his dogs

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion oversees proceedings in Liverpool as experts David Harper and Gary Pe help two teams buy their three items to take to auction

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid treats an acquaintance caught up in a workplace love triangle. Al decides to learn a new skill, while Ruhma receives bad news

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin revisits two sets of buyers who have successfully established new lives in the Devon countryside after appearing on Escape to the Country. First he is invited to the north of the county by an entrepreneurial couple whose rural aspirations needed to accommodate not only them but a pack of alpacas, paying guests and a grand piano. He then travels south to reunite with a couple at their picture-perfect home where they have found the space and time to indulge in their passions

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues another selection of items from the recycling centre, giving a little green chair to upholsterer Simion Hawtin-Smith and a dated display cabinet to upholsterer Katie Keat, while she transforms a pair of school chairs

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Auctioneers James Braxton and Charles Hanson seek treasure in Kent. Both plump for Japanese items, but it remains to be seen who will make the most profit

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: As Team Rickshaw complete another huge day of their challenge at Goodwood, Alex Jones and Michael Ball get the final live update from Matt Baker about how it's going, before the big finish on tomorrow's Children in Need

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kat and Phil head out for a cleaning job at the insurance company, but will Phil agree to take part? Tina asks Ian to give Mick a job behind the bar, hoping it will lighten his mood, although things only get worse when Shirley arrives. Jack and Denise take Raymond to his family church and are delighted to see the youngster happy

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team descend on Caswell Bay, Swansea, to transform a dilapidated, out-of-use bus shelter into the first, fully adapted surf centre Surfability UK, a charity that provides surfing lessons and experiences for people with disabilities and learning difficulties, and which receives funding from Children in Need. Throughout, Nick presents stories of how children and young people have benefitted from the support of the charity, but the challenge remains tough as this is the team's first build during Covid-19

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The four strongest chefs from the first two heats return for the quarter-final, inventing a dish that makes the most of an eclectic variety of spices. They then have to cook two courses for restaurant critics Jimi Famurewa and William Sitwell, who scrutinise the dishes in each and every detail. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in the next round

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former British number one tennis player Greg Rusedski, ex-England wicketkeeper Matt Prior, Great Britain sprinter Bianca Williams and former Bolton Wanderers striker Kevin Davies join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of terraced houses in Walthamstow in London and Dover in Kent, and a three-bedroom semi in the Essex town of Epping

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Two sisters who tried to cheat their insurers following a car crash. Plus, a suspiciously unlucky computer user caught out after making multiple claims for lost laptops. Presented by Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: The emergency services attend a baby who has cut his throat on a glass. Plus, a woman is knocked to the ground and trampled after a dog spooks horses. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In this Children in Need special, Natasha Raskin-Sharp looks back at some of the famous faces who have taken part in the annual charity initiative and finds out how Pudsey Bear was created. She also offers hints and tips about buying and selling antiques and collectables.

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers get to grips with a specially designed pram for messy eating, a super-absorbent sponge that can soak up 12 times as much water as normal, and an anti-theft sports bag

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson returns to the Isle of Wight, the location of a previous property hunt, wanting to discover whether a couple he helped succeeded in finding a balance of home and business potential in their new setting. Back on the mainland, Jules travels to south-west Wales to meet a husband and wife who craved a sizable Welsh farmhouse, but who have found that a home is about so much more than bricks and mortar

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Presenter Jay Blades enlists the help of designer Sarah Peterson to give two side tables a stylish new look, while lighting expert Duncan McKean attempts to give two old record players a radical overhaul. For his own project, Jay attempts to make some money for nothing from two farmhouse stools. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The open road brings Charles Hanson and James Braxton - and their classic cars - to Hampshire. Along the way, Charles struggles with the art of signwriting and James meets a little owl. James is also drawn to an exotic alcohol container, while Charles' eye lands on an unusual pocket watch stand. The duo then head to the final auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which five pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: A jam-packed evening of socially distanced entertainment, with exclusive sketches, exciting performances, music, dance and a few surprises. Mel Giedroyc is joined by Alex Scott, Chris Ramsey and Stephen Mangan, who will all be co-hosting the fund-raising telethon for the first time. There are Strictly Come Dancing, EastEnders and Doctor Who-related surprises in store, while Matt Baker, Ade Adepitan and the team of six young riders make their way to the finishing line after 332 miles around the Goodwood Estate as part of The One Show's 2020 Rickshaw Challenge. Beverley Knight and Shawn Mendes perform, while The Repair Shop team gives one very special girl a surprise to treasure for ever

programme.title: A Simple Favour (2018)
programme.desc: Stephanie Smothers, a widowed single mother who runs a crafts and recipes vlog, becomes friends with Emily Nelson, a public relations director for a fashion company. Emily has a work crisis and asks Stephanie to babysit her son, but later goes missing amid suggestions from colleagues she has jetted off to Miami. Feeling perplexed, Stephanie attempts to get to the bottom of her friend's mysterious actions. Paul Feig's comedy thriller based on Darcey Bell's novel, starring Anna Kendrick, Blake Lively and Henry Golding

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson meets two couples who have bought stately homes, including one family who have embarked on an ambitious restoration of a historic building in the Devon countryside

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore sees the profit-making potential in an old wooden bed at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey. Other recovered items include a chest of drawers and two tired chairs

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Gavin & Stacey co-creator and star Ruth Jones traces her family tree, learning that her paternal grandfather was a leading figure in the Medical Aid Societies of South Wales - which provided a model for the National Health Service in 1948. On her maternal side, she discovers that her great-great-great-grandfather was a mariner who rose to the rank of captain in the domestic coastal trade

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore a three bedroom semi in West Bromwich, a flat in Stoneycroft in Liverpool and a Grade II listed building in Whitehaven in Cumbria

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: A team of three players must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing's Len Goodman takes actor Ian Kelsey to Lyme Regis to relive a childhood holiday from 1973, including a boat trip and a game of pub cricket

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: With George and Smiler along for the ride, Woody makes his secret trip to Australia. However, there is a shocking surprise in store there for him. Back in Weld, Shay remains the object of Ollie's unwanted affections. Comedy drama, with Rick Donald

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: Judges Kate Malone and Keith Brymer Jones ask the remaining potters to make 10 identical long necked vases using the Raku technique. This Japanese method of decorating and firing dates back to the 16th century and requires the contestants to take their red hot vases straight out of the kiln and plunge them into a bin full of combustible materials. Sara Cox hosts

programme.title: Tigers About the House
programme.desc: Spot and Stripe are nine weeks old and are becoming quite a handful as they explore the Clarks' home in suburban Queensland. Giles feeds them meat, which brings out some very adult tiger behaviour, and encourages their development by allowing them outside for the first time. The zookeeper also visits the rainforest of Sumatra - the home of the tigers in the wild - to see first-hand the poaching and persecution the big cats are subjected to in their natural habitat

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Previously unseen finds from the show's travels on visits to Muncaster Castle in Cumbria, the Bowes Museum in Co Durham, the Grand Pier in Weston-super-Mare in Somerset and Norwich Cathedral. The antiques experts include Caroline Hawley, Kate Bateman, Thomas Plant, David Harper and Jonathan Pratt with items ranging from Black Forest carvings to a delicate oil painting completed on glass by the artist Bessie Bamber. Presenter Paul Martin also takes a look at the lavish interiors and the beautiful gardens at Powis Castle in mid-Wales

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Steve Backshall, Catherine Bohart, Dr Ranj Singh and Meera Syal test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball gives all the backstage gossip and exclusive news from Strictly Come Dancing, and Ian Waite gives his thoughts on the couples' training progress

programme.title: The Unknown Warrior 100
programme.desc: Huw Edwards presents highlights from Westminster Abbey of a unique event marking the 100th anniversary of the burial of the Unknown Warrior. An Armistice Day ceremony encompassing the national moment of remembrance at 11.00 reflected the original 1920 funeral of the Unknown Warrior, including a performance of the hymn Abide with Me, sung by Ruby Turner. Those in attendance included The Prince of Wales and The Duchess of Cornwall

programme.title: War Walks
programme.desc: The history of the 1914 Battle of Mons, when 100,000 British troops were forced to retreat into France by German forces equipped with machine guns and heavy artillery. Professor Richard Holmes charts the course of the conflict and follows in their footsteps as they withdrew south from Mons

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Following the stories of puppies and their owners, including border collie Meg, who has her work cut out for her as she trains to be a sheepdog. Elsewhere, cameras follow great dane Shelby, as he joins a young family which already includes Mr Cooper, a three-year-old dachshund

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Period drama set in London in the 1760s following Margaret Wells as she struggles to reconcile her roles as mother and brothel owner. The shock of the previous night's events reverberates around London. Meanwhile, rumours abound as suspicion spreads through Soho

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: A light-hearted look at the social and cultural changes society went through since the turn of the 21st century, featuring contributions from politicians and celebrities. In the fourth episode, Angela Scanlon, Nigel Harman and Rachel Parris take a light-hearted look back at the year 2003, from Beyonce's hit Crazy in Love, to David Blaine living in a box over the Thames

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The return of the documentary following the day-to-day realities the NHS faces, this time focusing on the impact of the pandemic at the Royal Free London, a world leader in the treatment of infectious diseases and one of the biggest NHS trusts in the country. In the first edition, the UK lockdown is over, but hospital staff are counting the cost of having to prioritise Covid-19 above almost everything else as they battle to treat the patients left behind

programme.title: Inside the Batcave
programme.desc: Lucy Cooke presents a documentary taking a journey into the secret world of one of the most endangered and least understood animals on Earth - bats. With cutting-edge night-vision cameras and ultrasonic detectors, this programme follows a greater horseshoe bat roost for four months, capturing the hidden life of the colony as never before, witnessing the birth of a new generation of pups and tracking their progress towards their perilous maiden flights outside the roost

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to Wales, taking a glamorous road trip in the newest grand tourers from Bentley, Aston Martin and McLaren, before taking on their German rivals and Formula One legend Heinz-Harald Frentzen. Plus, a tribute to Stirling Moss.

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Firefighters in Newcastle and Humberside are out on shift in the days leading up to Bonfire Night, the most dangerous time of the year. Officers from Merseyside Police attempt a dangerous rescue when a car gets stuck in the River Mersey with the driver trapped inside

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is in Dorset as she catches up with past house-hunters who have found a better quality of rural life near the coast

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades finds items to restore at Walsall Recycling Centre in the West Midlands, including a swivel chair and a sideboard

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming: Compilation
programme.desc: Mike and Michael are forced to call the police when a confrontation at a Yorkshire car dealership turns ugly. Can they get the man who was sold a faulty car the money he is owed? Rob and Gerald are called to remove a group of squatters from a family business. The unwelcome guests have racked up thousands of pounds in utility bills leaving the owner out of pocket and the place a mess. Tommy goes in search of a debtor on the beautiful Welsh coast and it seems a boat removal will be the most likely outcome

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode presents the alphabetical tour through the BBC's culinary archive, this time selecting recipes and ingredients linked by the letter E. Raymond Blanc demonstrates how to make chocolate eclairs, and Giorgio Locatelli and Tony Allan cook eels and mash

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len takes Janet Ellis back to the New Forest to reminisce about a family holiday the TV presenter and actress enjoyed there in the early 1960s. He starts by picking her up in a Ford Anglia Saloon, just like the one her family had back in 1963. As well as seeing the area's famous ponies and riding a rollercoaster, the duo also make and tuck into one of Janet's favourite holiday food memories - baked Alaska

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George's house is even more full of waifs and strays than usual, and the first step to him having some peace and quiet is to get Tracy to forgive Woody

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: The six remaining potters are challenged to make a garden sculpture out of slabs of clay and with a place in the semi-final at stake, their designs prove to be more ambitious than ever before. For their Spot Test, the hopefuls transform a chimney pot into a strawberry container, before trying to impress the judges by throwing the widest plate they can

programme.title: Tigers About the House
programme.desc: Spot and Stripe are outgrowing Giles Clark's home and developing adult behaviour, so the time comes for them to be reintegrated with the rest of the tigers at Australia Zoo. Their development is far from over, however, as they have their first swimming lesson and meet the adults they are going to share their lives with. But all is not well with Spot, who picks up a life-threatening eye condition

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team heads to Sandon Hall in Staffordshire, where Christina Trevanion and David Fletcher assess items including a large collection of boxed Corgi toys and some rare watches. Paul Martin discovers the dramatic political life of one of Sandon's earlier owners, who was a member of William Pitt's cabinet in tumultuous times

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Steve Backshall, Catherine Bohart, Ranj Singh and Meera Syal test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal dishes more backstage gossip and finds out what some of the couples will be wearing this weekend with the help of chief costume designer Vicky Gill

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: The Booker Prize 2020
programme.desc: Kit de Waal presents this programme profiling the six authors shortlisted for the Booker Prize, the most prestigious literary award in the English-speaking world. Kit also examines the wider landscape of the publishing industry in the wake of the unprecedented global events of 2020

programme.title: Saving Britain's Pubs with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: For the past 20 years, pubs have been closing down at an alarming rate. Concerned by a crisis in the industry he loves, Tom Kerridge sets out on a mission to revive struggling pubs. He begins at the White Hart in Chilsworthy, Cornwall, where a 300-year-old freehouse, the only pub in the village, is in danger of closing

programme.title: Gary Lineker: My Grandad's War
programme.desc: The Match of the Day presenter retraces the footsteps of his grandfather Stanley Abbs to explore a brutal but often overlooked chapter of the Second World War. Using the official war diary kept by Stanley's unit as a guide, Gary revisits the places where he was stationed and meets experts who help him piece together what happened to his grandfather during the Italian campaign. Along the way, Gary meets veterans who share first-hand testimonies to and help him understand the reality of combat in Italy

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark hosts this fundraising special in which Dara O Briain and Fay Ripley lead teams from the BBC and ITV. Competing with them are Anita Rani, Steve Pemberton, Iain Stirling, Joel Dommett, Charlene White and Dane Baptiste, but who has the knowledge to make it to the top of the class?

programme.title: The Mini Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and comedy correspondents Rachel Parris, Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on everything that happened in the week the programme first aired in May 2019

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Golf: The Masters
programme.desc: Kat Downes presents action from the first day of the Major, taking place at Augusta National in Augusta, Georgia. At the end of this stage in 2019, Bryson DeChambeau and then-reigning PGA Championship and US Open champion Brooks Koepka tied for the lead on six-under par

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael reflects on his fourth travel series, exploring the Sahara. This journey brought him into contact with nomads, refugees and artists who had sought out this vast desert, but also those displaced by war, the migrant crisis and the rise of militant Islam. The journey took Michael over the Atlas Mountains, to a Smara refugee camp, to the famed city of Timbuktu, and into the deep desert as part of a camel train

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: A light-hearted look at the social and cultural changes society went through since the turn of the 21st century, featuring contributions from politicians and celebrities. In this episode, Angela Scanlon, Ellie Taylor and Geoff Norcott take a light-hearted look back at the year 2000, from Kylie's iconic gold hotpants to Anne Robinson becoming TV's most feared woman

programme.title: Murder Case
programme.desc: Police Scotland's major investigation teams deal with around 60 murders a year and this edition delves deeper into the nature of the job. Through reconstructions and interviews with both senior and rookie detectives, the film reveals what it is like to work on such devastating cases as the murder of Alesha MacPhail and the disappearance of Julie Reilly.

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin revisits two sets of buyers who have successfully established new lives in the Devon countryside after appearing on Escape to the Country. First he is invited to the north of the county by an entrepreneurial couple whose rural aspirations needed to accommodate not only them but a pack of alpacas, paying guests and a grand piano. He then travels south to reunite with a couple at their picture-perfect home where they have found the space and time to indulge in their passions

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues another selection of items from the recycling centre, giving a little green chair to upholsterer Simion Hawtin-Smith and a dated display cabinet to upholsterer Katie Keat, while she transforms a pair of school chairs

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a set of bagpipes that once belonged to a man's grandfather, who served as a piper and a soldier in the Royal Scots during the First World War. Elsewhere, silversmith Brenton West helps to restore a Welsh crown of cultural significance that bears little resemblance to its former splendid self, clockwork genius Steve Fletcher takes on the repair of a quirky tortoise-shaped bell, and furniture restorer Will Kirk is thrilled to be presented with an enormous challenge - an antique wooden chair dating from the Carolean period

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode continues his alphabetical tour through the BBC's culinary archive, this time selecting recipes and ingredients linked by the letter G. Lorraine Pascale cooks pan-fried mascarpone gnocchi with dreamy basil pesto, and Rachel Khoo serves grapefruit meringue tartlets

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len takes Jake Wood back to the Cornish coast, where the EastEnders star reminisces about a school adventure holiday he had there in 1980

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: With the wedding back on, it's time for Woody's 'groomspiel', and Big Mac has wild plans for it. However, Woody has made Tracy a promise he'll behave - which is going to be hard to keep. Meanwhile, new arrival Poppy and Arlo are getting on so well that Lindsay is jealous, even if Arlo is too innocent to realise

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: The five remaining potters are faced with their most technically demanding challenge when they have to make a decorative chandelier in bone china. For their Spot Test, steady hands are required when judges Kate Malone and Keith Brymer Jones assess their 'banding' skills, and for the Throw Down they have just 15 minutes on the wheel to make the largest closed sphere they can

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: The four remaining potters compete in the final with Kate Malone and Keith Brymer Jones presenting them with their most technically demanding challenge in the Main Make when they have to make an original 12-piece tea set out of porcelain. For their Spot Test, they must cut intricate and decorative designs into leather hard porcelain using a technique known as 'the devil's work', and for the Throw Down they have just 20 minutes to make three high-shouldered jugs - one of the hardest shapes to throw at the wheel

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents previously unseen finds from the show's travels round the country, visiting Muncaster Castle, the Bowes Museum and Norwich Cathedral. Antiques experts include Kate Bateman, Paul Laidlaw, Jonathan Pratt, James Lewis and Thomas Plant, with items ranging from a pair of Lalique vases to a 9.5mm projector. Paul also takes a tour around Powis Castle in Wales, finding out about their collection of Indian artefacts housed on site in the Clive Museum

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Steve Backshall, Catherine Bohart, Ranj Singh and Meera Syal test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the week's ultimate winner finally declared

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal is joined by a trio of celebrity superfans to discuss all things ballroom. Gethin Jones is at Elstree Studios with the latest news from behind the scenes

programme.title: A Special School
programme.desc: Observational documentary following the reality of life in Britain's biggest special school. In the first episode pupils face their fears at an outdoor adventure centre. The focus of the trip is for the young people to have an opportunity to challenge their disability physically, and abseiling in a wheelchair epitomises this experience

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox invites actress and writer Meera Syal, rapper Guvna B, comedian Dave Gorman and newsreader Babita Sharma to discuss the books that take them back to their youth, the books that transport them to far-flung places and the books that make them feel positive about the world. They also discuss this week's book group pick, Love in Colour by Bolu Babalola, as well as Babita's book The Corner Shop

programme.title: Snow Cats and Me
programme.desc: Part one of two. Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan joins pioneering biologist Dr Victor Lukarevskiy in Russia to attempt to rehabilitate two pairs of lynx held captive for years. Two of these cats, Koshka and Miass, were born in the wild, but have been locked in tiny steel cages for years by a fur trapper, while their Bryansk and Dasha have never seen the wilderness, and Dr Lukarevskiy wants to give these lynx a new life in the wild. Will they have the instincts needed to survive in the wild? And after years in human captivity, will they make it?

programme.title: Royal History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The Regency rea is often regarded as genteel and well-ordered, with beautiful buildings, Jane Austen romance and red-coated officers defeating Napoleon at Waterloo. However, historian Lucy Worsley digs behind the facade of Georgian elegance to reveal the fibs that helped conceal a darker side to the Regency and suppressed rebellion in an age of revolution. She discovers that to quell rebellion, monarchy and government relied on spin, secrets and lies

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Contributors including Bonnie Tyler, Tracie Young, Marilyn and members of New Order, Kajagoogoo, Haysi Fantayzee, Spandau Ballet, the Smiths, Flying Pickets and Thompson Twins reflect on an era when the mood in the charts was escapism and musical reinvention. It was a difficult time for the Top of the Pops itself, as the rise of the video started to threaten its unique appeal - getting the day's top artists into the studio. This forced producer Michael Hurll to experiment with presenters and features - including dispensing with dance troupe Zoo

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories, presented by Faisal Islam

programme.title: Golf: The Masters
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the second day of the Major, taking place at Augusta National in Augusta, Georgia. Francesco Molinari and Brooks Koepka shared the lead at the end of this stage last year, with Tiger Woods one stroke behind as he bid to win the title for the fifth time in his career

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part one of two. Starbuck, Boomer and Helo return to the Astral Queen, where Roslin forces them on a desperate mission to find the Tomb of Athena. Adama appoints an inexperienced replacement for his son, who proves inadequate in almost everything he's asked to do - endangering the lives of the rest of the fleet in the process. Edward James Olmos stars

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part two of two. Adama reassembles the fleet and follows President Roslin to the surface of Kobol, where she searches for the tomb of Athena that will lead them to Earth. The estranged groups make peace and reunite to continue the expedition, but their destination holds an unusual surprise. Meanwhile, Meier resorts to drastic measures to ensure Zarek's rise to power

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Jane Corbin finds out how Liverpool coped with restrictions due to Covid-19 infection rates, hearing from residents worried about the second wave, business owners pushed to the brink of despair, and those who say that living under tier three rules just isn't possible

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A 75-year-old patient has an enlarged aorta, the main vessel for carrying oxygenated blood to the body. Her condition could cause her to die at any time - but treating it also poses a risk to her life. At Addenbrooke's hospital, a patient with a genetic condition that makes her susceptible to cancer undergoes treatment to remove a polyp that has the potential to turn into cancer

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: A look at the hidden secrets of the universe

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a cool blue waterway winding effortlessly from the far away high peaks and evergreens in a mountain landscape

programme.title: New York: America's Busiest City
programme.desc: Anita Rani, Ade Adepitan, Ant Anstead and Dan Snow look at food consumption in the Big Apple. From dusk until dawn at the New Fulton fish market in The Bronx, they uncover the hidden night time operations, hard-nosed negotiations and price fluctuations of this enormous wholesale operation. Ade also looks into local apple production, Anita visits a cattle farm that supplies the region's steakhouses, Ant visits the New NY Bridge, and Dan heads to Freshkills on Staten Island. Once the world's biggest landfill, it's been transformed into 2,200 acres of parkland

programme.title: Lost Films of WW2
programme.desc: Part two of two. Footage shot by an officer on HMS Kent during the Battle for the Mediterranean, and the D-Day landings filmed by a naval lieutenant on his personal camera

programme.title: Berlin 1945
programme.desc: Life in the German capital in the year the Second World War ended, told through the stories of those who experienced it - from ordinary Germans to the Allied forces who entered the city. In the final episode, the British, French and Americans troop wait to enter Berlin, while members of the Jewish community, among whom there are few survivors, regroup

programme.title: Walter's War
programme.desc: Drama based on the life of Walter Tull, a black professional footballer who enlists in the Army during the First World War. While recuperating at home from a bout of trench fever, Walter learns he has been recommended for officer training at Gailes, Ayrshire - but must overcome prejudice, as well as his own demons, before returning to the front. Starring OT Fagbenle, Ewan Stewart, Kwame Kwei-Armah and Dugald Bruce-Lockhart

programme.title: Walter Tull - Forgotten Hero
programme.desc: Former EastEnders actor Nick Bailey investigates the story of Walter Tull, the pioneering black British footballer who died heroically fighting in World War One. He examines how Walter became the first black officer in the British Army when regulations required men of pure European descent, asks why he was denied the honour of a Military Cross for heroism and explores how he overcame racial abuse

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a cool blue waterway winding effortlessly from the far away high peaks and evergreens in a mountain landscape

programme.title: Lost Films of WW2
programme.desc: Part two of two. Footage shot by an officer on HMS Kent during the Battle for the Mediterranean, and the D-Day landings filmed by a naval lieutenant on his personal camera

programme.title: War Walks
programme.desc: Richard Holmes traces the events of the Battle of the Somme in 1916, the blackest episode in British military history, when 57,000 Allied soldiers were killed or wounded in a matter of hours

programme.title: Berlin 1945
programme.desc: Life in the German capital in the year the Second World War ended, told through the stories of those who experienced it - from ordinary Germans to the Allied forces who entered the city. In the final episode, the British, French and Americans troop wait to enter Berlin, while members of the Jewish community, among whom there are few survivors, regroup

programme.title: War Walks
programme.desc: Richard Holmes provides an insight into the events of Operation Goodwood, the Allied forces' armoured break-out of the bridgehead in Normandy in 1944, revealing how a German officer's order resulted in the destruction of advancing British tanks. However, the battle - later described as the 'death ride of the armoured divisions' - succeeded in distracting enemy forces from a simultaneous American push

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an old weathered cabin buried deep in the snow and lined with protective evergreens

programme.title: On the Town (1949)
programme.desc: Musical following three sailors who are out for a good time while on a day's shore leave in New York. One tries initially to fend off the advances of a female taxi driver, another is pulled into helping with an anthropologist's study of prehistoric man, while the third seeks out the model in a famous advertising campaign - but finds she is not quite what he expected. With Gene Kelly, Frank Sinatra and Jules Munshin

programme.title: The Queen Mary: Greatest Ocean Liner
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the history of the Clyde-built ship, from the maiden voyage in May 1936 to New York, and her 1939 role in the Battle of the Atlantic, to her current status as a floating luxury hotel and museum in Long Beach, California. Among the contributors are singer Johnny Mathis

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the hard-working culture that existed in the Clyde shipyards, which were responsible for making the Queen Mary and QE2, despite some of the harshest working conditions in industrial history and dire industrial relations. The programme examines how the employees' work ethic and strength of public support led them to defy the then-government's efforts to close the factories down in 1971 and win the right to carry on shipbuilding

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: For decades Margaret Atwood has been universally acclaimed as Canada's greatest living writer and her star shines brighter and bolder than ever, thanks to the TV adaptation of her dystopian novel The Handmaid's Tale. Alan Yentob meets the author in Toronto to discover how a childhood spent between the wilderness and the city helped shape her vision of herself and the world, set alight her imagination and set her forth on a path to literary success

programme.title: The Trouble with Naipaul
programme.desc: Documentary in which Shahidha Bari examines the cultural legacy of writer VS Naipaul, exploring how his controversial views on race and history of violence influence how his books are seen through modern eyes. The programme takes a look at the abuse he suffered in childhood, and how it influenced his attitude to the country of his birth, Trinidad and Tobago

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an old weathered cabin buried deep in the snow and lined with protective evergreens

programme.title: War Walks
programme.desc: Richard Holmes provides an insight into the events of Operation Goodwood, the Allied forces' armoured break-out of the bridgehead in Normandy in 1944, revealing how a German officer's order resulted in the destruction of advancing British tanks. However, the battle - later described as the 'death ride of the armoured divisions' - succeeded in distracting enemy forces from a simultaneous American push

programme.title: 21st Century Mythologies with Richard Clay
programme.desc: The art historian explores some of the everyday myths of 21st-century popular culture, revealing the hidden meanings in everything from money, Wi-fi and race to the Madonna. Along the way, he meets avant-garde artists including Clet Abraham, Ingrid Burrington, Molly Soda and Rene Matic, and looks at semiotics, the study of signs and symbols that provides an analytical toolkit to helps navigate advertising and its demands for attention

programme.title: The Live Lounge Show
programme.desc: Clara Amfo peeks behind the scenes of Radio 1's Live Lounge, the twice-weekly feature attracting an array of some of the biggest and best pop artists on the planet. Here, she features Miley Cyrus, Jorja Smith and Little Mix

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Simon Mayo hosts the edition first aired on March 15, with performances by artists including Inspiral Carpets, Primal Scream, Candy Flip, New Kids on the Block, the Mission, the B-52's, Beats International featuring Lindy Layton and Jive Bunny and the Mastermixers

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first aired on March 22, with performances by artists including Orbital, Snap!, Big Fun, Erasure, 49ers, David A Stewart featuring Candy Dulfer, Jimmy Somerville and Beats International featuring Lindy Layton

programme.title: Ella Fitzgerald: Just One of Those Things
programme.desc: The life and times of the jazz singer, beginning with a 1934 talent contest at the Apollo Theatre in Harlem, and following her next five decades, reflecting the passions and troubles of the times in which she lived in her life and her music. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo, with contributions from Laura Mvula, Ray Brown Jr, Tony Bennett, Johnny Mathis, Jamie Cullum, Cleo Laine and Smokey Robinson

programme.title: Ella Fitzgerald Sings
programme.desc: A performance from 1965 by the jazz singer accompanied by the Johnny Spence Orchestra, including big band versions of Don't Rain on My Parade and Mack the Knife

programme.title: Jazz Divas Gold
programme.desc: A selection of archive performances by acclaimed female jazz artists from shows including Not So Much a Programme - More a Way of Life, Morecambe and Wise, Parkinson and Show of the Week. Featuring Ella Fitzgerald, Peggy Lee, Blossom Dearie, Nina Simone, Cleo Laine, Marion Montgomery, Betty Carter, Sarah Vaughan, Eartha Kitt and Amy Winehouse

programme.title: Radio 2 in Concert

programme.title: Mimi & Co
programme.desc: In the last episode of the series, Mimi enlists her brother Leo’s help to fulfil a lifelong dream: to win a Blue Peter badge. She teaches Leo to rollerblade, they upcycle a mirror together and draw portraits of each other, only to discover they are too old to apply for a badge! We meet proud badge holders Iris and Aidan, and find out all about the Chinese Mid- Autumn Festival, which they celebrate with their mum and dad. Luckily for Mimi, Blue Peter’s very own Mwaksy invites Mimi to co-host Fan Club Live, and there is a special surprise for her in the post.

programme.desc: A sardine swallows the key to a treasure chest, and humpback whale Kwazii tracks it down

programme.title: Alphablocks

programme.title: Justin's House

programme.title: Tiny Wonders
programme.desc: The tide is out, and there’s lots to see as the Noggle lands on the seashore. There must be a Tiny Wonder to explore. Yapp-Yapp makes a discovery that surprises all the Nogglins.

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say 'boots' prior to going on a whirlwind word adventure

programme.title: Hey Duggee

programme.title: Tik Tak

programme.title: Olobob Top

programme.desc: Ferne visits Chloe and her pet dog Willow as they prepare to go to the vet to get Willow's teeth cleaned and Rory heads to a zoo to meet a scaly resident

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Sister and brother, Isla and Noah take their giant bubble wands on a walk up a steep hill. It's very windy at the top of the hill - perfect for making bubbles as big as your head!

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie visits a bowling alley to find out how it works, and we learn how ribbon is made

programme.title: Swashbuckle

programme.title: Hey Duggee

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: When Jen accidentally grinds up some giraffe thorn tree seeds that Mr Hammond wanted Andy to plant, Andy must head to the Namibian desert in Southern Africa and track some down - and what better way to do that from the top of a giraffe itself? However, when another male giraffe arrives on the scene, a fight ensues and Andy is caught in the middle

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Everyone is practising their acts for the big talent show, but the curtain won't go up or down and Bob is feeling nervous about performing in front of everyone. So while Bitz tries to get the curtain working, Bob has to try to overcome his nerves and keep the audience happy with a song. Thankfully Purl is there to help and the show, and curtain, are a huge success

programme.title: Go Jetters

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog

Narrated by Emma Willis

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. First to arrive at the barn are sisters Christine Hopkins and Pauline Samways from Kidderminster, who bring in a miniature bicycle built by their grandfather. Elsewhere, silversmith Brenton West takes receipt of a small mosaic crucifix with an extraordinary story, and Steve Fletcher turns his attention to an army issued box kite. Finally, antique toy enthusiast David Burville sets to work restoring a Victorian acrobat toy

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Four more professional chefs arrive from across the nation to compete. In the second round, the four chefs must impress the judges with two stand out Signature Dishes of their own creation, before two of the competitors are sent home

programme.title: My Family, the Holocaust & Me with Robert Rinder
programme.desc: Part one of two. The barrister helps the second and third generations of British Jewish families affected by the Holocaust to retrace their relatives' footsteps and discover the full truth about what happened to them. Robert begins by meeting two British Jewish families who want to investigate what happened to their relatives, as well as exploring his own family's stories on both his mother's and his father's sides

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin revisits two sets of buyers who have successfully established new lives in the Devon countryside after appearing on Escape to the Country. First he is invited to the north of the county by an entrepreneurial couple whose rural aspirations needed to accommodate not only them but a pack of alpacas, paying guests and a grand piano. He then travels south to reunite with a couple at their picture-perfect home where they have found the space and time to indulge in their passions

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues another selection of items from the recycling centre, giving a little green chair to upholsterer Simion Hawtin-Smith and a dated display cabinet to upholsterer Katie Keat, while she transforms a pair of school chairs

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Auctioneers James Braxton and Charles Hanson seek treasure in Kent. Both plump for Japanese items, but it remains to be seen who will make the most profit

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: As Team Rickshaw complete another huge day of their challenge at Goodwood, Alex Jones and Michael Ball get the final live update from Matt Baker about how it's going, before the big finish on tomorrow's Children in Need

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kat and Phil head out for a cleaning job at the insurance company, but will Phil agree to take part? Tina asks Ian to give Mick a job behind the bar, hoping it will lighten his mood, although things only get worse when Shirley arrives. Jack and Denise take Raymond to his family church and are delighted to see the youngster happy

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team descend on Caswell Bay, Swansea, to transform a dilapidated, out-of-use bus shelter into the first, fully adapted surf centre Surfability UK, a charity that provides surfing lessons and experiences for people with disabilities and learning difficulties, and which receives funding from Children in Need. Throughout, Nick presents stories of how children and young people have benefitted from the support of the charity, but the challenge remains tough as this is the team's first build during Covid-19

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The four strongest chefs from the first two heats return for the quarter-final, inventing a dish that makes the most of an eclectic variety of spices. They then have to cook two courses for restaurant critics Jimi Famurewa and William Sitwell, who scrutinise the dishes in each and every detail. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in the next round

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former British number one tennis player Greg Rusedski, ex-England wicketkeeper Matt Prior, Great Britain sprinter Bianca Williams and former Bolton Wanderers striker Kevin Davies join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Cookbook
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Simon King travel to the Germanic town of Swakopmund, on the Namibian coast, to experience a traditional barbecue - or braai. To find the right spot among the dunes, the motorcyclists take to the air in a microlight

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Two sisters who tried to cheat their insurers following a car crash. Plus, a suspiciously unlucky computer user caught out after making multiple claims for lost laptops. Presented by Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: The emergency services attend a baby who has cut his throat on a glass. Plus, a woman is knocked to the ground and trampled after a dog spooks horses. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In this Children in Need special, Natasha Raskin-Sharp looks back at some of the famous faces who have taken part in the annual charity initiative and finds out how Pudsey Bear was created. She also offers hints and tips about buying and selling antiques and collectables.
